promotion_argument_for_override: The raw cap miss is exactly one FP.
The extra one can be attributed to case 125 object_not_found placeholder accounting on a positive case; that is already represented by a false negative and is not an extra hallucinated target.
The main replay improved recall materially versus the worktree adopted-runtime all-current replay: +7 matches and -7 false negatives.
Positive controls 155 and 166 passed, and office-negative abstention passed.
No broad group/scene-box regression was found in the FP-bearing cases.
promotion_argument_against_override: Dense-formation case 67 remains severe, with 10 FPs and 10 FNs.
Case 66 still over-enumerates dense-row fragments/debris even while matching all references.
Cases 12/90 show a repeated duplicate/split-building pattern.
Raw bda_eval gate remains a fail until the user explicitly accepts semantic FP accounting or changes the cap.
summary.recommended_next_action: Ask user to approve the one-FP semantic override, then commit the prompt-only main config change; otherwise restore backup or continue review.

case_reviews.finding: The correct large damaged building is matched, but the model adds a second left-edge building/subsection box that duplicates part of the grouped building scene rather than a separate reference target.
case_reviews.promotion_impact: Counts as a real extra target, but it is a bounded duplicate/split-building precision issue, not a broad scene-box failure.

case_reviews.finding: The model appears to look near the tank but places the box too high and labels the target as no-damage, producing a paired FN/FP.
case_reviews.promotion_impact: A real localization/assessment failure, but not evidence of runaway target enumeration.

case_reviews.finding: The main destroyed building is matched well; the model adds an intact background/adjacent building as an extra no-damage building.
case_reviews.promotion_impact: A real extra target, but limited and not a group/scene box.

case_reviews.finding: The destroyed tank is matched well; the model adds a nearby building at the upper right as an extra target.
All eight reference trucks are recovered, but the model also emits four small near-road/front-row boxes over debris or ambiguous row fragments.
case_reviews.promotion_impact: This is the clearest real FP cost of the prompt: recall is excellent, but dense-row over-enumeration remains.

case_reviews.finding: The model locks onto a row of dust/cloud/smoke positions and only matches the large foreground vehicle; ten distant reference vehicles are missed while ten unsupported row boxes are emitted.
case_reviews.promotion_impact: Largest remaining blocker and the main caution against treating v017b as solved. It is a known hard dense-formation failure, not a new broad group-box regression.

case_reviews.finding: Same visual pattern as case 12: the grouped severe-damage building reference is matched, with an extra left-side partial building/subsection box.
case_reviews.promotion_impact: Real duplicate/split-building FP, bounded to a repeated building-fire image pattern.

case_reviews.finding: The foreground armored vehicle is detected with duplicate/partial boxes, while small distant convoy vehicles are missed.
case_reviews.promotion_impact: Real mixed failure, already present in the runtime-adoption replay; not the marginal +1 cap miss.

case_reviews.finding: The model abstains with object_not_found even though the current v2 reference is a destroyed military-equipment target. bda_eval counts that placeholder as an FP in addition to the FN.
case_reviews.promotion_impact: This is the decisive +1 over the FP cap under raw bda_eval accounting. Semantically, it is a recall/abstention failure already counted as an FN, not a hallucinated extra target.

source_manifest.purpose: Review the 22 raw bda_eval false positives from the v017b prompt-only main all-current/no101 replay that missed the FP cap by 1.
source_manifest.candidate: v017b_group_box_rejection promoted prompt text in main config, uncommitted
forbidden_mutations_confirmed: runtime config beyond pending pre-existing main prompt edit
